description: Both were adult birds and males. I will describe the first. It was a bird of about 10 inches in length, with large eyes with yellow iris and dark centers. The face had a dark edge to the facial disk. Forehead had white spots. Breast was white with brown streaks. Wings were brown with some white spotting. Bill was pale like the color of a fingernail. The birds had responded to my playing of the staccato song of the Boreal Owl on a tape player. This was to be the first of many observations of Boreal Owls for me as I documented a number of these from Spring Creek Pass to Slumgullion Pass.  Before too long I developed a system for photographing them at Spring Creek to Slumgullion Pass. I want to get these observations entered even if it is 40 years after. I am using my notes from those owling trips to develop this report and locations.
